metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_3124
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Abstract: "We have used a retroviral cDNA expression system to drive the expression of the different forms of glutamic acid decarboxylase (GAD65, GAD67, or both). Individual clones of engineered Rat-1 cells make the appropriate GAD mRNAs and GAD polypeptides, show GAD enzymatic activity, and make GABA."

Note:
- GAD's glutamate decarboxylase activity converts L-Glutamate into 4-aminobutanoate (GABA).
- The neurotransmitter role of GABA in the CNS is well established.
- This activity is not shown in vivo in any known publications known at the time of this writing.. Experimental description: - Overexpressed GAD1; GAD67 is of rat species.
